skill.name: autofix
skill.description: Auto-fix CodeRabbit review comments - get CodeRabbit review comments from GitHub and fix them interactively or in batch
skill.summary: Auto-fix CodeRabbit review comments by fetching them from GitHub and applying fixes interactively or in batch mode
skill.author: coderabbitai

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. Static scanner flagged 71 potential issues but all are false positives. External commands detected are legitimate GitHub CLI operations (gh pr list, gh api graphql, git push) with hardcoded arguments. No user input injection vectors found. SHA references are content hashes for integrity verification, not cryptographic encryption. Network access is limited to official GitHub API endpoints. No prompt injection detected. Skill is safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Usage for GitHub Integration
low_findings.description: Skill uses shell commands (gh CLI, git) for GitHub integration. These are hardcoded operations with no user input injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands use $(git branch --show-current) which is safe - gets current branch name only, no external input. All gh commands use hardcoded operations against authenticated GitHub CLI.

content.value_statement: Code reviews generate actionable feedback but fixing each issue manually takes time. This skill fetches CodeRabbit review threads from GitHub and applies fixes automatically with your approval.
actual_capabilities: Fetch unresolved CodeRabbit review threads from GitHub PRs via GraphQL API
Parse review comments to extract issue metadata, severity, and fix instructions
Apply code fixes based on CodeRabbit agent prompts with user approval
Create consolidated commits for all applied fixes
Post summary comments to GitHub PRs documenting changes made
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) installed and authenticated
Only processes threads from CodeRabbit bot accounts
Does not fix issues that span multiple unrelated files in single comment

use_cases.title: Streamline Code Review Workflow
use_cases.description: Developers with open PRs reviewed by CodeRabbit can quickly address feedback without manually copying issues to separate prompts.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working with CodeRabbit on GitHub

use_cases.title: Batch Fix Low-Priority Issues
use_cases.description: Senior developers can run auto-fix mode to handle minor issues like formatting or naming conventions while focusing on architectural decisions.
use_cases.target_user: Tech leads managing code quality across teams

use_cases.title: Automated Code Quality Maintenance
use_cases.description: CI pipelines can notify developers of pending CodeRabbit reviews and guide them to use the skill for efficient fix application.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers setting up development workflows

prompt_templates.title: Quick Fix Single Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Fix the most critical CodeRabbit review issue on your current PR

prompt_templates.title: Review All Issues First
prompt_templates.scenario: See all unresolved CodeRabbit threads before deciding what to fix

prompt_templates.title: Auto-Fix All Applicable Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Automatically apply all fixes without per-issue approval

prompt_templates.title: Create PR if Missing
prompt_templates.prompt: Get CodeRabbit review for my current branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a new PR and wait for CodeRabbit to review it

output_examples.output:
Found 3 CodeRabbit review threads:

| # | Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|----------|-------|----------|
| 1 | CRITICAL | Missing null check | src/auth.py:42 |
| 2 | HIGH | Inefficient query | src/db.py:89 |
| 3 | MEDIUM | Unused import | src/utils.py:15 |

Which would you like to fix?

Applying fixes...

✅ Fixed: Missing null check at src/auth.py:42
✅ Fixed: Inefficient query at src/db.py:89
✅ Fixed: Unused import at src/utils.py:15

Created commit: abc1234
Push changes? [Yes/No]

best_practices: Push commits before running autofix so CodeRabbit has reviewed the latest code
Use review mode first to understand what CodeRabbit recommends before auto-applying
Follow AGENTS.md instructions if your repo has them loaded by the skill
anti_patterns: Do not run autofix with uncommitted changes - they will not be in the CodeRabbit review
Do not skip build and lint checks before pushing fixes to avoid breaking CI
Do not defer all fixes repeatedly - technical debt will accumulate in the PR

faq.question: What happens if CodeRabbit has not reviewed my code yet?
faq.answer: The skill will inform you that the review is in progress and suggest trying again in a few minutes.

faq.question: Can I fix only specific issues instead of all of them?
faq.answer: Yes. Choose the review mode to see all issues first, then approve or skip each fix individually.

faq.question: What if my repo has an AGENTS.md file?
faq.answer: The skill will load AGENTS.md instructions at the start and follow them throughout the workflow.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code without my approval?
faq.answer: Manual review mode shows each fix before applying it. Auto-fix mode applies all fixes but you can still cancel before pushing.

faq.question: What if the fix breaks my code?
faq.answer: The skill prompts you to run build and lint checks before pushing. Review changes in review mode before applying them.

faq.question: Which CodeRabbit bot names are supported?
faq.answer: The skill filters for threads from coderabbitai, coderabbit[bot], and coderabbitai[bot].
